name: Estate Solutions, LLC
pain_points: Site is heavily JavaScript-rendered — content is invisible to crawlers/bots (terrible for SEO)
No extractable text content on homepage despite HTTP 200 response
Meta description mentions 24/7 service, IICRC certified, 60-min response, direct insurance billing — but none of this is visible in page body
Phone number (404-913-3030) only appears in meta tags, not verifiable in page content
Cannot confirm presence of contact forms, CTAs, or booking tools
Cannot confirm mobile responsiveness from content extraction
Restoration/damage niche (water, fire, storm) — not a remodeling company
notes: Site exists but is poorly built for SEO — JS-only rendering means Google may not index content. 92 reviews at 4.8 stars shows strong business. Restoration niche may differ from remodeling leads but still home services.

name: Ben Hill Renovations
pain_points: Original URL is HTTP (not HTTPS) — redirects to HTTPS but starting point is insecure
Very content-heavy homepage could overwhelm visitors
Testimonials section only shows one review despite 232 Google reviews
notes: Excellent professional site built on Next.js. Has scheduling/appointment forms with city+service selection, showroom, featured projects gallery, trust badges (TrustDALE), testimonials, phone number prominent. 232 reviews at 4.8 is outstanding. In business since 1964. This company does NOT need our help — their web presence is top-tier. Skip.

name: All Weather Renovations
pain_points: Only 38 reviews — lower count compared to competitors despite quality site
Homepage is extremely long with SEO-heavy content (may hurt user experience)
Content reads like it was written primarily for search engines, not humans
Financing section is overly detailed for a homepage
Multiple duplicate forms on the same page (3 estimate request forms)
notes: Very professional WordPress site with strong SEO strategy. Multiple lead capture forms, TrustDALE certified with $10k guarantee, financing options, video content, project gallery, brand partnerships (TimberTech, AZEK, IKO). Based in Douglasville. Only weakness is lower review count (38). Site is comprehensive — they don't need a rebuild.

name: Elite Remodeling
pain_points: Website looks template/AI-generated (ZipWP image in logo path suggests auto-generated site)
Very minimal content — no detailed service pages visible from homepage
No trust badges, certifications, or awards displayed
No project gallery or before/after photos on homepage despite having great review photos
Logo is a low-quality placeholder image (zipwp-image-7682)
No financing information despite being a remodeling company
Missing meta description entirely
No service area map or detailed coverage info
Phone number present but no click-to-call optimization visible
Contact page exists but no inline forms on homepage
Reviews on the site are embedded Google reviews — no curated testimonial section
113 reviews at 4.9 stars is EXCELLENT but the website does not reflect this quality
Massive gap between review quality and website quality — perfect lead candidate
notes: This is the #1 lead candidate. Elite Remodeling has outstanding reviews (4.9 stars, 113 reviews) with detailed, glowing testimonials about Diego and his team. But their website is clearly auto-generated with ZipWP, uses placeholder assets, lacks a gallery, has no trust signals, and minimal content. The gap between their real reputation and their web presence is enormous. A professional site showcasing their actual work photos from reviews would be transformative. Based in Douglasville — perfect market match.

name: Small Jobs by Shelby Construction
pain_points: No website at all
No online presence to evaluate
No review data provided
notes: No website. Automatic Build candidate — needs a complete web presence from scratch.
